channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force awards GE, Rolls-Royce for ‘medium thrust’ drone engines | Breaking Defense
item.description: The two engine makers will advance designs to power medium thrust class drones, an Air Force spokesperson told Breaking Defense.

item.title: 🕵️ Should we be worried about the bond market? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Bond yields have spiked, especially in advanced economies. But why is that? And what does that actually mean? Alice Fulwood, US business editor at the Economist and co-host of the podcast Money Talks, joins Josh and Jessie to decode the signals in the global bond market—from…

item.title: 🏛️ Marine Corps’ Harrier completes final flight following SOUTHCOM deployment | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Marine Corps is moving through its Tactical Aircraft Transition Plan as it plans to fully adopt fifth-generation aircraft across the entire fleet.

item.title: 🕵️ Reading between the lines of Trump’s new executive order on AI | Atlantic Council
item.description: Atlantic Council experts dig into the details of US President Donald Trump’s recently signed executive order on artificial intelligence. The post Reading between the lines of Trump’s new executive order on AI appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 The New Weapon Behind Ukraine’s Battlefield Success | Foreign Policy
item.description: Mid-range drones that can hit targets almost 100 miles behind Russian lines have changed the game.

item.title: 🌏 Will China and the United States Pursue Strategic Stability? | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2024, Michael Swaine wrote, “How to Stop the United States and China from Sliding into War,” where he identified areas that could increase the possibility of an armed conflict between the United States and China. Two years later, after recent talks between President Trump and…

item.title: 📍 The biggest industry, training hurdles for making drone wingman a reality | Breaking Defense
item.description: In our final video looking at manned-unmanned teaming, we consider likely changes ahead for defense manufacturers as the US increasingly partners with drones in combat.

item.title: 🏛️ The Hidden History of America’s Harrier Jump Jets | The War Zone
item.description: The Harrier story is a transatlantic saga of improvisation, ambition, and unlikely partnership, which helped shape the F-35B. The post The Hidden History of America’s Harrier Jump Jets appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🛡️ Uzbekistan’s New Migration Destination? America. | The Diplomat
item.description: From irregular border crossings to bilateral training agreements, Uzbekistan is working to carve out a formal pathway to U.S. employment.

item.title: 🕵️ Trump's choice of acting intel chief threatens fragile surveillance-powers deal | Defense One
item.description: If William Pulte's appointment is not rescinded, Senate Democrats say, it could sink an extension of FISA Section 702.

item.title: 🌍 Anduril teams with Elbit America for Army’s self-propelled howitzer competition | Breaking Defense
item.description: Anduril joins the US subsidiary of Israeli company Elbit along with OshKosh Defense for the team’s offering for the highly anticipated competition.

item.title: 🏴 Russia’s Baltic Fleet Successfully Attacked By Ukrainian Drones | The War Zone
item.description: Once considered a sanctuary for Russian naval power, the Baltic Fleet is now in the crosshairs of Ukraine’s expanding long-range strike campaign. The post Russia’s Baltic Fleet Successfully Attacked By Ukrainian Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Congress Prepares for a Consequential Iran Vote | Foreign Policy
item.description: Tehran may feel empowered to wait out U.S. demands for concessions in face of war-weary American public.

item.title: 🌏 North Korea Sees a New Solar Power Push | 38 North
item.description: The recent opening of a new solar farm in Haeju, South Hwanghae province, marks a turning point ... The post North Korea Sees a New Solar Power Push appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 🏴 US tells Europe, Canada to boost NATO air and naval forces | Defense News
item.description: “Nations just need to assign the capabilities they have to NATO,” a spokesman for the alliance's military headquarters said.

item.title: 📍 Can Section 301 effectively replace IEEPA? That is the $166 billion question | Atlantic Council
item.description: Citing forced labor concerns, the Trump administration has proposed new tariffs on imports from sixty economies—a first step toward rebuilding its tariff wall. Still, the outcome is far from certain. The post Can Section 301 effectively replace IEEPA? That is the $166 billion…

item.title: 🌏 The U.S. and Taiwanese Militaries Can’t Really Fight Together | Foreign Policy
item.description: A joint campaign to defend the island would struggle to operate effectively.

item.title: 🌏 Trump’s New AI Order Raises the Stakes in China-US Tech Competition | The Diplomat
item.description: Advanced AI models are no longer treated simply as commercial products; they are increasingly regarded as strategic assets linked to national power.

item.title: 📍 Lockheed’s GRIZZLY C-UAS system downs attack drone in live-fire demo | Defense News
item.description: The live-fire demonstration paired the radars, Sanctum software and launcher to destroy the target at Yuma Proving Ground, Arizona.

item.title: 🌍 Gulf tensions escalate as Iran hits Kuwait, US strikes near Hormuz | Defense News
item.description: CENTCOM said it had carried out a new round of defensive strikes in southern Iran, while an Iranian drone attack on the Kuwait airport killed one person.

item.title: 🏛️ Army, J-7 to test new sensor with high-altitude balloon in coming days | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Wallabee prototype is set to provide the Army and the J-7 with more intelligence-gathering capabilities in the stratosphere.

item.title: 📍 America Enabled the Gulf’s African Adventurism | Foreign Policy
item.description: Why Washington let its Middle Eastern partners wreak havoc in Africa.

item.title: 📍 The US is delaying weapon sales to allies. Will there be long-term impact? | Breaking Defense
item.description: A number of factors may “lead even long-standing allies and partners to question whether or not it’s wise to continue to depend on the United States for essential defense articles and services,” one analyst said.

item.title: 🏴 Neutralizing Chokepoints: Lessons From the Hormuz Strait, Malacca, and Baltic Sea | The Diplomat
item.description: Insights from Pekka Virkki.

item.title: 🌍 EBRD Dials Down Kyrgyz GDP Forecast Over Sanctions Worries | The Diplomat
item.description: While still growing, Kyrgyzstan’s GDP may slow due to increasing weight of sanctions.

item.title: 🌏 Can a Takaichi Government Stabilize China-Japan Relations? | The Diplomat
item.description: What Abe's post-2014 rapprochement with Beijing can teach Tokyo today.

item.title: 🏛️ The USMC’s AV-8B Harrier Has Flown Off Into The Sunset | The War Zone
item.description: The Harrier Sundown ceremony marks the end of more than 50 years of Marine Corps jump jet operations. The post The USMC’s AV-8B Harrier Has Flown Off Into The Sunset appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine and the Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression: Redefining International Justice | Just Security
item.description: The tribunal to prosecute Russia's crime of aggression seeks to close one of the most enduring gaps in international criminal law and strengthen the U.N. Charter. The post Ukraine and the Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression: Redefining International Justice appeared…

item.title: 📍 Exit Polls Suggest Ruling Democratic Party’s Victory in Local Elections | The Diplomat
item.description: Exit polls suggest the DP will win 11 of 16 regional posts, with Seoul and Busan producing the night’s most closely watched results.

item.title: 🌍 Syria’s Accountability Gap: The Najib Trial and the Case for the ICC | Just Security
item.description: The first criminal trial against a senior Assad regime official comes at a time when Syria’s legal framework remains fragile. The post Syria’s Accountability Gap: The Najib Trial and the Case for the ICC appeared first on Just Security.
